name: Chocolate Ambrosia
recipeInstructions:
For the Cake: Place hot water and cocoa powder in the bowl of a mixer with a paddle. Mix until blended. Add mayonnaise. Blend until creamy. Add sugar, baking soda, and mix well. Add the flour in three batches, mixing only until blended. Pour into a pan that has been sprayed with cooking spray. Bake at 350°F for 30-40 minutes. Yield: 1 9-inch layer cake.
For the Ganache: Bring cream to a boil. Remove from heat. Whisk in coconut rum and chopped chocolate. Stir until all the chocolate has melted and set aside to cool.
To Assemble: Once the cake has cooled, use a slicing knife to even out the top of the cake. From this one layer, make four servings. Spread a thin layer of ganache on the bottom layer, top with a layer of mandarin oranges that have been drained from juice and patted as dry as possible, a layer of freshly grated coconut, and another thin layer of ganache. Repeat the process with the remaining 3 layers. Coat the top of the cake with a thin layer of ganache and garnish with mandarins and shaved chocolate.
